text:
## Quality Audit Results

**Count: 1 issue**

| Issue ID | Title | PRs Claiming to Close It |
|---|---|---|
| **ENG-142** | Primary DB failover is slow | #1007 (open, acme/infra), #1036 (open, acme/web) |

### Explanation

ENG-142 is marked **done** in Linear, and two pull requests claim to close it — but both PRs (#1007 and #1036) are still in **open** state, not merged. So the issue was marked done without any merged PR actually closing it.

The other 4 done issues were either properly closed by a merged PR (ENG-134) or had no PR claiming to close them at all (ENG-122, ENG-125, ENG-131), so they fall outside the audit scope.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(def merged-prs (filter #(= "merged" (:state %)) all-prs))
(def pr-closes (map #(vector (:closes_linear %) %) merged-prs))
(def done-ids (map :id done-issues))

;; Find which done issue IDs appear in any merged PR's closes_linear
(def closed-by-pr
  (set (filter #(contains? (set done-ids) %)
               (map first (filter first pr-closes)))))

(map (fn [issue]

             merged-closed? (contains? merged-closes-ids id)
             pr-closed? (contains? all-pr-closes-linear id)]
         {:id id
          :title (:title issue)
          :has-pr-claim pr-closed?
          :has-merged-pr merged-closed?}))
     done-issues)"}
